mission.title: grpc: [grpcio>=1.68.0][Backup Poller] Client fails to reconnect after server restart & Sporadic connection failures due to 'ping timeout'
mission.description: [grpcio>=1.68.0][Backup Poller] Client fails to reconnect after server restart & Sporadic connection failures due to 'ping timeout'. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Look for error: `UNKNOWN: ipv6:%5B::1%5D:50051: Failed to connect to remote host: Timeout occurred: FD Shutdown"`

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
Check your grpcio version with `pip show grpcio`. This bug affects grpcio versions 1.68.0 through 1.73.0. Versions 1.67.1 and earlier are not affected. The issue manifests as the client failing to reconnect after a server restart, with backup poller connection state handling errors.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [grpcio>=1.68.0][Backup Poller] Client fails to reconnect…
steps.description:
This bug was fixed in grpcio v1.73.1 and v1.72.2. Upgrade your grpcio package to one of these versions:
```bash
# Upgrade to latest fixed version
pip install --upgrade grpcio>=1.73.1

# Or pin to the backport version
pip install grpcio==1.72.2
```

steps.title: Upgrade grpc to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade grpc:
Update the grpc dependency in your project manifest to the latest version.
See the project's releases: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/releases

steps.title: Confirm [grpcio>=1.68.0][Backup Poller] Client fails to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that `UNKNOWN: ipv6:%5B::1%5D:50051: Failed to connect to remote host: Timeout occurred: FD Shutdown"` no longer appears.

resolution.summary: The backup poller reconnection bug was fixed in grpcio v1.73.1 (latest) and backported to v1.72.2. The root cause was in the backup poller's connection state handling after server restarts. See releases: v1.73.1 (https://pypi.org/project/grpcio/1.73.1) and v1.72.2 (https://pypi.org/project/grpcio/1.72.2).
codeSnippets:
# Upgrade to latest fixed version
pip install --upgrade grpcio>=1.73.1

# Or pin to the backport version if you need the 1.72.x line
pip install grpcio==1.72.2

prerequisites.description: gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
